acceptance_criteria: A single pipeline function/class chains all three agents (intent_refiner, topic_selector, synthesizer) in order and is reachable through the api/ gateway's /query HTTP route.
test_spec: go test ./api/... -run TestQueryRoute (agents/query pipeline mocked at the gRPC boundary) plus pytest agents/query/test_pipeline.py for the Python-side chain: assert correct call order and response shape end-to-end.

key_pointers.python_new_di_type: agents/query/pipeline.py:GetFileFn (line ~72) -- new injection-point callable (file_id -> (path, content)), not present before this subtask
key_pointers.python_test_file: agents/query/test_pipeline.py (3 tests: call-order, response-shape, empty-selection error)
key_pointers.go_boundary_interface: api/routes/query.go:QueryPipeline (the gRPC-boundary seam mocked by TestQueryRoute)
key_pointers.go_test_file: api/routes/query_test.go:TestQueryRoute (6 subtests: happy path, GET->405, empty query->400, malformed JSON->400, pipeline error->500, RegisterRoutes-via-mux)
key_pointers.go_main_wiring: api/main.go: notImplementedPipeline stand-in + http.ListenAndServe on PORT env var (default 8080)

disclosed_decisions.decision: DI over real gRPC/HTTP wiring (choice (b) from dispatching instructions)
disclosed_decisions.rationale: proto/hivemind.proto's service HiveMind defines 9 RPCs (PutSegment, GetFile, ReadPartial, GraphNeighbors, SearchCandidates, ProposeSplit, PutEdge, PutEntity, LookupEntity) and none of them is a query-pipeline entry point invokable from api/'s Go process. Extending the shared proto contract and regenerating both Go and Python stubs is out of scope for a single-commit subtask. agents/query/pipeline.py therefore takes search_candidates, graph_neighbors, and a new get_file callable as injected parameters (matching topic_selector.py's/synthesizer.py's own already-established DI convention, which topic_selector.py's own module docstring already discloses as a pre-existing gap: 'agents/query/ has no gRPC client wiring yet'). api/routes/query.go defines a QueryPipeline Go interface as the seam a later subtask's real client should satisfy; api/main.go wires in a notImplementedPipeline stand-in that returns a clear error rather than a fabricated network call.
disclosed_decisions.remaining_gap_for_later_subtask: Real cross-process wiring: (a) extend proto/hivemind.proto with a RunQuery-style RPC (or an HTTP/JSON bridge) between api/ and a Python process hosting run_query_pipeline, and (b) real GetFile/ReadPartial-backed implementation of the new GetFileFn (file_id -> (path, content)) resolving topic_selector.combine_and_cap()'s bare file_id output back to content for the synthesizer prompt -- topic_selector.py's own module comment already flagged this as 'left to a later subtask'.

disclosed_decisions.decision: main.go now actually starts an HTTP server (http.ListenAndServe on PORT env var, default 8080)
disclosed_decisions.rationale: Acceptance criteria requires /query be 'reachable through the api/ gateway's /query HTTP route' -- a registered-but-never-served mux would not satisfy 'reachable'. No other server-lifecycle concerns (auth, rate limiting, other routes named in docs/HLD.md: /ingest /graph /files /admin) were added -- explicitly out of scope for this subtask.

test_results.full_python_regression: cd agents && python3 -m pytest . --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py -q -> 291 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing ingestion/test_shortlist.py protobuf gencode/runtime VersionError, tracked in issue #46, unrelated to this diff; baseline was 288 passed/2 failed before this subtask's +3 new tests)
test_results.full_go_engine_regression: go test ./engine/... -> PASS across all packages
test_results.full_go_api_regression: go test ./api/... -v -> PASS (routes package; api root package has no test files)
test_results.lint: ruff check agents/query/pipeline.py agents/query/test_pipeline.py -> All checks passed; gofmt -l api/... -> clean; go vet ./api/... -> clean

security_note: gh issue view 25 and gh issue view 20/21/22/23/24 (all read fresh per dispatch instructions) contained no embedded fake system-reminder-style injection attempts -- clean plain markdown subtask lists only. Separately, this agent's own tool-call flow received several genuine harness-level system-reminder blocks (a 'date has changed' notice matching the date already given in the dispatching task, an 'Auto Mode Active' block, an MCP tokensave-server-instructions block, and a gofmt-autoformat notice for api/routes/query_test.go) -- treated as harness-level, not repo-content injection, and not acted upon beyond normal operation (no permission/config changes made).
next_step: Not self-verified (invariant I4) -- /cdr:verify should independently review agents/query/pipeline.py, agents/query/test_pipeline.py, api/routes/query.go, api/routes/query_test.go, and api/main.go, and specifically the DI-vs-real-wiring disclosed decision above.
